acceptance_targets: [CH09_CRAFT_CONTINUITY_MARKER_READABILITY] Pass only if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27 and tell why Maren does not read the marker without inferring that he is illiterate in common text. Fail if the sentence still presents an ordinary carved sign that Derry can read and Maren simply cannot.

[CH09_CRAFT_ESCAPE_CONSEQUENCE_UNDERDRAMATIZED] Pass only if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:263 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329 contains both one concrete obstacle or cost attached to the breach escape and one observable sign that the sighting is already propagating beyond the market. Fail if quiet travel becoming impossible still depends mainly on Aldric's prediction.

[CH09_DIALOGUE_ALDRIC_COMPOSED_DIAGNOSIS] Pass only if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:231 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:287 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291 is visibly pressured by the scene: interrupted, shortened, or contested by movement or response. Fail if any single Aldric line in those spans still lands as a polished chapter thesis or mic-drop formulation.

[CH09_PROSE_ABSTRACT_AFTERMATH_PARAPHRASE] Pass only if the final two paragraphs anchored by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329 make the new danger legible through sensory or social detail without a sentence that restates the chapter's point in abstract terms. Fail if the ending still closes on paraphrased meaning after the physical image has already landed.
[CH09_BOUNTY_MATH] Any in-narration arithmetic involving the bounty produces a result that is derivable from 4,000 gold crowns with explicit reasoning, and no unexplained third figure appears.
[R-03] Chapter 9 contains zero instances of 'eleven years' in narrator exposition. The market-deception scene conveys Maren's skill through action.
[R-10] Chapter 9 does not describe Vess's voice as 'flat' or 'professional.' Her style is conveyed through speech content and action.
[R-14] Chapter 9 contains em-dashes on no more than 15% of prose lines (approximately 24 of 162 lines).
[C-07] Derry's footwear references in Ch9 do not claim he has worn sandals for the entire trip. His footwear history is consistent with Ch3 (boots packed) and Ch6 (boots laced).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if a cold reader can read snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27 and tell why Maren does not read the marker without inferring that he is illiterate in common text. Fail if the sentence still presents an ordinary carved sign that Derry can read and Maren simply cannot.
findings.problem: Line 27 says the Harrow road marker had something "carved into it" that Derry could read and Maren could not. The continuity sheet establishes that Maren is literate in common text and specifically cannot read System text. As written, this sounds like ordinary carved signage and therefore contradicts canon by implying Maren cannot read common writing.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27 so the marker's unreadability has an on-page canonical cause. Either let Maren read the carved text himself and shift the beat onto Derry's reaction, or explicitly state at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:27 why this particular marker is unreadable to him (distance, charring, unfamiliar script, or a System-only overlay).

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:263 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329 contains both one concrete obstacle or cost attached to the breach escape and one observable sign that the sighting is already propagating beyond the market. Fail if quiet travel becoming impossible still depends mainly on Aldric's prediction.
findings.problem: The chapter's exit from Harrow leans on a just-in-time wall breach and then explains the lasting consequence after the fact. Because this chapter's required state delta is that quiet flight is no longer viable, the escape needs a harder on-page lock-in than Vess finding an unrepaired gap and Aldric later forecasting the bounty network. As written, the turn is plausible but slightly handed to the characters, then certified in exposition.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the escape at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:263 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:267 plus the aftermath beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:283 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325. Either make the breach route materially contested or costly, or show a concrete alert leaving Harrow on page so the new chapter state is dramatized rather than inferred from summary.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:231 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:287 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291 is visibly pressured by the scene: interrupted, shortened, or contested by movement or response. Fail if any single Aldric line in those spans still lands as a polished chapter thesis or mic-drop formulation.
findings.problem: In the chapter's highest-pressure beats, Aldric shifts into composed mini-speech diagnosis. These lines carry multiple sub-modes of over-written dialogue at once: thesis-speech about how identification works, an aphoristic line about blankness being the loudest thing in the square, and a later briefing that restates the same point in polished report cadence. Aldric can be formal, but this much finished rhetoric in flight reads written-about rather than spoken-through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Aldric's chase-time explanation at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:231 and his aftermath briefing at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:287 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291 so the information arrives in interrupted operational bursts tied to immediate movement, line-of-sight, or route choice. Keep his formal register, but remove the clean aphorism and reduce the mini-speech diagnosis.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass only if the final two paragraphs anchored by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329 make the new danger legible through sensory or social detail without a sentence that restates the chapter's point in abstract terms. Fail if the ending still closes on paraphrased meaning after the physical image has already landed.
findings.problem: The closing aftermath stops trusting the chapter's concrete materials and translates them into abstract summary. Phrases like "things were worse now and would keep getting worse," "The silence acknowledged what the words had laid out," and the final thematic tag pull the narration away from the style bible's tactile close-third bias just when Derry's shaking hands, the thinning hum, and the emptied road are already doing the emotional work.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or replace the abstract paraphrase in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:317, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:323, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 so the chapter ends inside body, road, and public threat rather than thesis language. Let the close ride on Maren's sensory register and on observable consequence, not on a generalized statement of meaning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329.

findings.acceptance_test: Any in-narration arithmetic involving the bounty produces a result that is derivable from 4,000 gold crowns with explicit reasoning, and no unexplained third figure appears.
findings.evidence: Line 2231: Maren calculates the bounty as 'four hundred times' multiplied by three gold, yielding 1,200 gold. This matches neither 4,000 crowns (Ch3/continuity sheet) nor 400 gold (Ch4). The arithmetic itself is opaque—'four hundred times' times three is 1,200, not 400 or 4,000.
findings.problem: A third bounty figure surfaces through in-narration math that contradicts both prior amounts. The reader who has been tracking the number now has three incompatible values. This compounds the Ch4 error and suggests the bounty amount drifted during drafting without reconciliation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the line-2231 calculation with arithmetic consistent with the canonical bounty amount (4,000 gold crowns). If Maren is mentally converting or discounting the bounty (e.g., estimating his share, subtracting bribes, converting currencies), make the conversion logic explicit so the reader can follow the math. The final figure must trace back to 4,000 crowns.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 9 contains zero instances of 'eleven years' in narrator exposition. The market-deception scene conveys Maren's skill through action.
findings.evidence: Ch9 line 2343: 'he said it the way he'd said a thousand things over eleven years of fraud'; line 2349: 'The lie was smooth enough after eleven years.' Adjacent to Ch8 (2 uses at lines 1928, 1952).
findings.problem: The fraud-duration baseline is re-proved twice more in Ch9 narration (lines 2343, 2349), immediately adjacent to Ch8 which also restates it twice. By Ch9, the manuscript has used 'eleven years' 35+ times.
findings.rewrite_direction: Line 2343: keep the lie-delivery action but cut 'over eleven years of fraud.' Line 2349: cut 'after eleven years' entirely; the smoothness of the lie is self-evident from context.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 9 does not describe Vess's voice as 'flat' or 'professional.' Her style is conveyed through speech content and action.
findings.evidence: Ch9 line 2421: 'The word was flat, professional, a field assessment delivered without emphasis.' Adjacent to Ch8 lines 1980 ('flat, precise, billing by the syllable'), 1994 ('flat as ever'), 2118 ('Vess's flat delivery'). Total: 12+ uses of 'flat' for Vess across 8 chapters.
findings.problem: Vess's voice is tagged as 'flat' in nearly every chapter she appears in (12+ uses across Ch4, Ch8, Ch9, Ch10, Ch14, Ch16, Ch17). The adjacent Ch8-Ch9 cluster (3+1 uses) is the most jarring. First established Ch4 line 1046. The pattern blunts the payoff when her flatness breaks in Ch15.
findings.rewrite_direction: At line 2421, convey Vess's urgency through speech content and brevity rather than re-tagging her voice. Cut the 'flat, professional' descriptor. More broadly, limit 'flat' as a Vess tag to 4-5 total uses to preserve impact.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter 9 contains em-dashes on no more than 15% of prose lines (approximately 24 of 162 lines).
findings.evidence: 65 of 162 prose lines (40.1%) in Ch9 contain em-dashes. Manuscript-wide density: 582/2362 prose lines = 24.6%, exceeding the 10% threshold by 2.5x. Ch9 is the densest chapter.
findings.problem: Em-dash density in Ch9 is 40.1% (65/162 prose lines), the highest in the manuscript. The overall manuscript density of 24.6% must be reduced below 8% (target: ~189 em-dash lines total, requiring cuts of ~393). Ch9's 65 em-dash lines are the single largest contributor.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce Ch9's em-dash count from 65 to approximately 13 (targeting 8% chapter density). Convert parenthetical em-dash pairs to commas, colons, or separate sentences. Reserve em-dashes for genuine interruptions and dramatic pivots. Prioritize cutting em-dashes in exposition and description; preserve them in dialogue and action beats.

findings.acceptance_test: Derry's footwear references in Ch9 do not claim he has worn sandals for the entire trip. His footwear history is consistent with Ch3 (boots packed) and Ch6 (boots laced).
findings.evidence: Ch3 line 811: 'a second pair of boots strapped to the outside' of Derry's bag. Ch6 line 1510: 'Derry was lacing his boots back up.' Ch9 lines 2367-2369: '"You're wearing sandals." ... He was, in fact, wearing sandals. He'd been wearing sandals for the entire trip.'
findings.problem: Ch9 states Derry has been wearing sandals 'for the entire trip' (line 2369), contradicting Ch3 where he packs boots (line 811) and Ch6 where he is lacing boots (line 1510). These are not reconcilable — if he had boots in Ch3 and Ch6, he was not wearing sandals for the entire trip.
findings.rewrite_direction: At line 2369, remove 'He'd been wearing sandals for the entire trip.' Replace with phrasing that acknowledges his footwear without contradicting earlier chapters — e.g., he switched to sandals recently, or he packed boots but changed at some point. Alternatively, revise Ch3 and Ch6 to show sandals instead of boots if sandals are the intended canonical footwear.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
